title: Pretraining: Learning Grammar
concept: Pretraining (via masked language modeling or next-word prediction) teaches the model grammar, syntax, and general facts from web-scale data.

exercises.prompt: How does a model learn spelling and grammar on its own?

options.text: Predicting the next word in a sentence

exercises.explain: Next-word prediction is the core task that teaches models how sentences are built and what facts look like.

cards.title: Learning to Read
cards.body: Before an AI can write essays or write code, it has to learn how language actually works. How does it learn the difference between 'there', 'their', and 'they're' without a human teacher? It starts with pretraining!

cards.title: What is Pretraining?
cards.body: Pretraining is the first phase of training an AI. By reading massive amounts of text from the internet, the AI learns spelling, grammar, facts, and how words connect to each other.

cards.title: The Baby Listener
cards.body: Imagine teaching a baby to talk by letting them listen to millions of conversations. They don't have a teacher pointing at rules, but by hearing 'the dog runs' instead of 'dog the runs' over and over, they naturally learn how sentences are built.

cards.title: Predicting the Blanks
cards.body: If you feed an AI the sentence: 'The cat sat on the ___', pretraining teaches it that 'mat' or 'floor' makes sense, but 'banana' or 'running' does not fit the grammar and context.

cards.title: No Grammar Teachers Required
cards.body: People think AI is trained by humans explaining every rule of grammar. Actually, the AI figures out grammar all on its own just by predicting missing words in billions of web pages!

cards.title: The Training Journey
cards.body: See how the focus changes before and after an AI is pretrained.

right.text: Learns to follow specific human prompts and act as a helpful assistant.

left.text: Reads billions of web pages to learn basic grammar, spelling, and general facts.

exercises.prompt: What kind of data does an AI read during the pretraining phase?

options.text: Massive amounts of general web and book text
A small list of math formulas only
A dictionary of definitions written by hand
Private emails sent between two people

exercises.explain: Pretraining is done using web-scale data, which includes books, articles, and websites, to learn general patterns.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Pretraining happens before the AI is taught to follow specific human instructions.
exercises.explain: Pretraining uses massive datasets to help the AI learn general language rules before it ever learns to follow specific instructions.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing step name.
exercises.sentence: The process of learning general language rules from web data is called _______.
exercises.explain: Pretraining is the initial phase where the AI reads web-scale data to pick up basic language structure and facts.

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs shows an AI simply predicting the next words based on pretraining?

options.text: Hello! I am a helpful customer service agent. How can I help you today?
Once upon a time, there was a little girl who lived in a cabin in the...
Error 404: Page not found. Please check the URL.

exercises.explain: The AI is predicting the next word naturally based on patterns it learned, which is typical of a pretrained model. The other options show specific task training or hardcoded responses.

exercises.prompt: Why is pretraining the very first step in training a large language model?

options.text: It teaches the AI to follow strict safety rules and guidelines.
It trains the AI on specific private customer datasets.
It helps the AI learn general grammar, facts, and structure from web text.
It makes the AI perfectly accurate and unable to make mistakes.

exercises.explain: Pretraining is all about learning the foundation—like grammar, spelling, and facts—by looking at massive amounts of general text from the internet!
